articles.title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
articles.description:
In January, five incumbents were unseated, adding seven new faces earning the $15,000 raise. Here's what they're doing with it.

The post ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Quincy City Councilors are accepting a $15,000 pay raise despite pledging to repeal it.
Seven new council members, elected in January, are benefiting from the raise.
This decision may affect public trust in local government accountability.

articles.title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
articles.description:
"A deal constructed around Jarren Duran should remain within reach."

The post Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Red Sox identified as top trade candidate for Isaac Paredes.
Trade proposal could involve Jarren Duran as a key player.
Potential trade aims to strengthen Red Sox lineup for upcoming season.

articles.title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
articles.description:
“Burst and elusiveness are on Howell’s rush menu and he pairs it with a refreshing inside spin counter."

The post New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: ESPN's mock draft predicts Patriots will select pass-rusher Cashius Howell in 2026.
Howell, known for his burst and elusiveness, played at Texas A&M.
This selection could enhance the Patriots' defense, benefiting local fans and the team's performance.

articles.title: Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say
articles.description:
A man who was previously serving a sentence for attempted murder has been found guilty of killing the college student in 1986.

The post Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A man was convicted for the 1986 murder of a Salem State student.
The convicted man was already serving time for attempted murder.
This conviction brings closure to the victim's family after 40 years.

articles.title: N.H. woman accused of civil rights violation after allegedly shooting at lost man because he was Black
articles.description:
Diane Durgin, 67, is accused of shooting at a Black man who inadvertently drove to her property after a prearranged truck part sale, prosecutors said.

The post N.H. woman accused of civil rights violation after allegedly shooting at lost man because he was Black appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Diane Durgin, 67, allegedly shot at a Black man on her property in New Hampshire.
The incident occurred during a prearranged truck part sale, highlighting racial tensions.
This case raises concerns about civil rights and safety for Boston's diverse communities.

articles.title: Small plane makes emergency landing in frigid Hudson River and the 2 people aboard swim to safety
articles.description:
The plane's two occupants were a flight instructor and his 17-year-old student, New York State Police said Tuesday.

The post Small plane makes emergency landing in frigid Hudson River and the 2 people aboard swim to safety appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Small plane made emergency landing in Hudson River, occupants swam to safety.
Two people aboard: a flight instructor and his 17-year-old student.
Incident highlights risks of small aircraft training near Boston's waterways.
